title: Cloud Networking Overview
content: Networking is the connective tissue of the cloud. It defines how your resources talk to each other and how users reach your applications. In the cloud, networking is software-defined, giving you total control via APIs.
insight: Networking is no longer about cables; it's about configuration.

title: The VPC
content: A VPC is a logically isolated virtual network in your cloud account. You define its IP address range (using CIDR blocks), create subnets, configure route tables, and set up network gateways.
insight: Your VPC is the 'fence' around your cloud resources.

title: CIDR Blocks
content: Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) is a method for allocating IP addresses. A VPC CIDR block like 10.0.0.0/16 provides 65,536 IP addresses.
insight: Choose a large enough CIDR block to allow for future growth.

title: Subnets
content: A subnet is a range of IP addresses in your VPC. You launch resources like EC2 instances into a specific subnet. Each subnet must reside entirely within one Availability Zone.
insight: Subnets allow you to segment your network for security and organization.

title: Public Subnets
content: A public subnet has a direct route to an Internet Gateway. Resources in this subnet can have public IP addresses and are reachable from the internet.
insight: Public subnets are for 'front-facing' resources like load balancers.

title: Private Subnets
content: A private subnet does not have a direct route to an Internet Gateway. Resources in this subnet only have private IP addresses and are protected from direct internet access.
insight: Private subnets are the safest place for your databases and internal logic.

title: Route Tables
content: A route table contains a set of rules (routes) that are used to determine where network traffic is directed. Each subnet in your VPC must be associated with a route table.
insight: Route tables are the 'GPS' for your network packets.

title: Internet Gateway
content: The IGW allows communication between instances in your VPC and the internet. It performs network address translation (NAT) for instances that have been assigned public IP addresses.
insight: The IGW is the 'front door' of your VPC.

title: NAT Gateway
content: A NAT Gateway lives in a public subnet and allows instances in a private subnet to connect to the internet while preventing the internet from initiating a connection with them.
insight: NAT Gateways provide secure outbound-only internet access.

title: Security Groups (SGs)
content: SGs act as a virtual firewall for your instances. They are stateful, meaning if you allow an inbound request, the outbound response is automatically allowed.
insight: SGs control traffic at the instance level.

title: Network ACLs
content: NACLs are an optional layer of security for your VPC that acts as a firewall for controlling traffic in and out of one or more subnets. They are stateless.
insight: NACLs control traffic at the subnet level.

title: VPC Peering
content: VPC peering allows you to connect two VPCs (in the same or different accounts/regions) so they can communicate using private IP addresses as if they were on the same network.
insight: Peering is the easiest way to connect separate virtual networks.

title: VPC Endpoints (PrivateLink)
content: VPC endpoints enable you to privately connect your VPC to supported cloud services (like S3 or DynamoDB) without requiring an Internet Gateway or NAT Gateway.
insight: Keep your traffic on the cloud provider's private backbone for security.

title: Transit Gateway
content: A Transit Gateway acts as a central hub that connects VPCs and on-premises networks. It simplifies your network topology as you scale to many VPCs.
insight: The 'Router in the Cloud' for complex, multi-VPC architectures.

title: Cloud DNS (Route 53)
content: Route 53 is a highly available and scalable cloud Domain Name System (DNS) web service. It translates human-readable names like example.com into numeric IP addresses.
insight: DNS is how users find your application in the vast cloud.

title: Alias Records
content: Alias records are a Route 53-specific extension to DNS. They allow you to point your domain name directly to cloud resources like CloudFront distributions or Load Balancers.
insight: Use Alias records to point to AWS resources instead of CNAMEs for better performance.

title: DNS Health Checks
content: Route 53 can monitor the health of your resources. If a resource becomes unhealthy, DNS can automatically stop sending traffic to it and failover to a healthy resource.
insight: Route 53 ensures users are only sent to working servers.

title: Simple Routing
content: The most basic routing policy, where you point a domain name to a single resource (e.g., one IP address).
insight: Best for single-server setups.

title: Weighted Routing
content: Weighted routing allows you to associate multiple resources with a single domain name and choose how much traffic is routed to each (e.g., for A/B testing).
insight: Split your traffic between different versions of your app.

title: Latency Routing
content: Latency routing directs user requests to the region that provides the lowest latency for that specific user.
insight: Automatic global performance optimization.

title: Failover Routing
content: Failover routing is used for active-passive configurations. Traffic is sent to the primary resource unless it's unhealthy, in which case it goes to the secondary.
insight: The foundation of DNS-based disaster recovery.

title: Geolocation Routing
content: Geolocation routing lets you choose the resources that serve your traffic based on the geographic location of your users (e.g., send European users to a specific server).
insight: Tailor your application's reach based on where your users live.

title: Geoproximity Routing
content: Geoproximity routing lets Route 53 route traffic to your resources based on the geographic location of your users and your resources.
insight: More complex than Geolocation; allows for 'biasing' traffic toward certain regions.

title: Multi-Value Answer Routing
content: Multi-value answer routing lets you configure Route 53 to return multiple values, such as IP addresses for your web servers, in response to DNS queries.
insight: A simple way to achieve client-side load balancing and high availability.

title: Elastic Load Balancing (ELB)
content: ELB automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets. It handles the varying load of your application traffic in a single AZ or across multiple AZs.
insight: Load balancers prevent any single server from becoming a bottleneck.

title: CloudFront
content: CloudFront is a CDN service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers globally with low latency and high transfer speeds.
insight: Speed up your global application by caching content at the edge.

title: Global Accelerator
content: AWS Global Accelerator uses the AWS global network to direct traffic to your optimal regional endpoint based on health, client location, and policies.
insight: Provides static IP addresses that act as a fixed entry point to your global application.

title: AWS Direct Connect
content: Direct Connect is a cloud service solution that makes it easy to establish a dedicated network connection from your premises to AWS. It bypasses the public internet.
insight: For consistent, high-bandwidth connections between your data center and the cloud.

title: Site-to-Site VPN
content: AWS Site-to-Site VPN creates a secure connection between your data center or branch office and your AWS VPCs over the public internet using IPsec.
insight: A fast and encrypted way to connect your office to the cloud.

title: AWS Client VPN
content: A managed client-based VPN service that enables you to securely access your AWS resources and resources in your on-premises network from anywhere.
insight: Secure remote access for your developers and staff.

title: Elastic Network Interface (ENI)
content: An ENI is a logical networking component in a VPC that represents a virtual network card. It can include IP addresses, a MAC address, and security group memberships.
insight: Instances can have multiple ENIs for complex networking setups.

title: Enhanced Networking
content: Enhanced networking uses single root I/O virtualization (SR-IOV) to provide high-performance networking capabilities (higher I/O and lower CPU usage).
insight: For when your application needs the absolute highest network throughput.

title: Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA)
content: An EFA is a network interface for EC2 instances that enables customers to run applications requiring high levels of inter-node communications at scale on AWS.
insight: Used for High Performance Computing (HPC) and machine learning workloads.

title: VPC Flow Logs
content: VPC Flow Logs is a feature that enables you to capture information about the IP traffic going to and from network interfaces in your VPC.
insight: The 'security camera' for your network traffic.

title: Traffic Mirroring
content: Traffic Mirroring allows you to copy network traffic from an ENI of an EC2 instance and send it to security and monitoring appliances for deep packet inspection.
insight: Useful for advanced network forensics and troubleshooting.

title: IPv6 in VPC
content: VPCs and subnets can support both IPv4 and IPv6. IPv6 addresses are globally unique and do not require NAT for outbound internet access.
insight: Prepare for the future by enabling IPv6 in your cloud networks.

title: Egress-Only IGW
content: An egress-only internet gateway is a horizontally scaled, redundant, and highly available VPC component that allows outbound communication over IPv6 from instances in your VPC to the internet.
insight: The IPv6 equivalent of a NAT Gateway.

title: Reachability Analyzer
content: A configuration analysis tool that enables you to perform connectivity testing between a source resource and a destination resource in your VPC.
insight: Automated troubleshooting for 'Why can't Server A talk to Server B?'.

title: AWS Cloud Map
content: Cloud Map is a cloud resource discovery service. It allows you to name your application resources and it keeps track of the changing locations of these dynamically changing resources.
insight: Service discovery for modern, dynamic microservices.

title: Conclusion
content: Cloud networking provides the foundation for building secure, scalable, and global applications. By mastering VPCs, DNS, and load balancing, you can architect systems that are both resilient and performant.
insight: A well-designed network is the secret to a secure and fast cloud application.
